children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Commandant, and such other individuals and organizations as the Commandant considers appropriate, shall develop a training course on the workings of Congress.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The training course developed in subparagraph (A) shall be offered at least once each year.

children.heading: .—Additional training
children.label: ii .—Additional training
children.text: The training developed under subparagraph (A) may be provided more than once a year to facilitate timely receipt by covered recipients.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: At least once each year, any covered recipients shall receive the training developed under paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: flag officers serving in the Coast Guard;

children.label: ii
children.text: members of the senior executive service (career reserved) serving in positions in the Coast Guard; and

children.label: I
children.text: serving in positions in the Coast Guard; or

children.label: II
children.text: at the Department of Homeland Security with Coast Guard in their portfolio, including any Senior Advisor to the Secretary for the Coast Guard.

children.label: A
children.text: the history and structure of Congress and the committee systems of the House of Representatives and the Senate, including the functions and responsibilities of the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the documents produced by Congress, including bills, resolutions, committee reports, and conference reports, and the purposes and functions of such documents;

children.label: i
children.text: the congressional budget process;

children.label: ii
children.text: the congressional authorization and appropriation processes;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Senate advice and consent process for Presidential nominees; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the Senate advice and consent process for treaty ratification;

children.label: v
children.text: all relevant notification and reporting requirements in statute, policy, or any other agreement to Congress;

children.label: D
children.text: the roles of Members of Congress and congressional staff in the legislative process; and

children.label: E
children.text: the concept and underlying purposes of congressional oversight within the governance framework of separation of powers;

children.label: F
children.text: the roles of independent oversight entities, including the Offices of the Inspector Generals, the Government Accountability Office, and other independent entities, with respect oversight of the Coast Guard;1So in original. Probably should be followed by “to”.1

children.label: G
children.text: the legal and ethical requirements of complying with oversight conducted by such independent oversight entities, including compliance with congressionally mandated oversight;

children.label: H
children.text: an overview of (popularly known at the Privacy Act of 1974) with respect to working with Congress and independent oversight;section 552a of title 5

children.label: I
children.text: an overview of the right of all Coast Guard members and staff to engage with Congress as a constitutionally protected right; and

children.label: i
children.text: compliance with such law and policy;

children.label: ii
children.text: ethics;

children.label: iii
children.text: professionalism; and

children.label: iv
children.text: timeliness of response to Congressional oversight requests, including requests from independent oversight entities.

children.heading: Training for Congressional Affairs Personnel.—
children.label: b Training for Congressional Affairs Personnel.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Commandant shall develop a training course on the workings of Congress, which shall be administered in person for to any required participant.2So in original.2

children.label: i
children.text: a fellow;

children.label: ii
children.text: a liaison;

children.label: iii
children.text: a counsel; or

children.label: iv
children.text: administrative staff;

children.label: B
children.text: a Coast Guard district or area governmental affairs officer;

children.label: C
children.text: an individual who reviews, makes edits, or transmits formal or informal correspondence with respect to the Coast Guard to Congress, including relevant program level personnel;

children.label: i
children.text: the Office of Coordination, Programs, and Accountability or successor office; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the Force Design 2028 office; and

children.label: iii
children.text: Coast Guard Office of General Law personnel, including such personnel detailed to the Coast Guard.

children.label: i
children.text: the items described in subparagraphs (C) through (K) of subsection (a)(2);3So in original. Probably should be “subparagraphs (C) through (J) of subsection (a)(3);”.3

children.label: ii
children.text: the roles of Coast Guard fellows, liaisons, counsels, governmental affairs officers, the Coast Guard Office of Program Review, the Coast Guard Headquarters program offices, and any other entity the Commandant considers relevant;

children.label: iii
children.text: the roles and responsibilities of Coast Guard public affairs and external communications personnel with respect to Members of Congress and the staff of such Members necessary to enhance communication between Coast Guard units, sectors, and districts and Member offices and committees of jurisdiction so as to ensure visibility of Coast Guard activities; and

children.label: I
children.text: compliance with such law and policy;

children.label: II
children.text: ethics;

children.label: III
children.text: professionalism; and

children.label: IV
children.text: timeliness of response to Congressional oversight requests, including requests from independent oversight entities.

children.heading: Detail within coast guard office of budget and programs.—
children.label: 4 Detail within coast guard office of budget and programs.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: At the written request of a receiving congressional office, the training course required under this section shall include a multi-day detail within the Coast Guard Office of Coordination, Programs, Accountability to ensure adequate exposure to Coast Guard policy, oversight, and requests from Congress.4So in original. Probably should be followed by “and”.4

children.heading: .—Nonconsecutive detail permitted
children.label: B .—Nonconsecutive detail permitted
children.text: A detail under this paragraph is not required to be consecutive with the balance of the training.

children.heading: .—Completion of required training
children.label: 5 .—Completion of required training
children.text: A member of the Coast Guard selected for a position described in subsection (a) shall complete the training required by this section before the date on which such member reports for duty for such position.

children.heading: .—Outside experts
children.label: 1 .—Outside experts
children.text: The Commandant shall ensure that not less than 60 percent of the lecturers, panelists, and other individuals providing education and instruction as part of the training courses required under this section are experts on Congress and the Federal legislative process who are not employed by the executive branch of the Federal Government.

children.heading: .—Authority to accept pro bono services
children.label: 2 .—Authority to accept pro bono services
children.text: In satisfying the requirement under paragraph (1), the Commandant shall seek, and may accept, educational and instructional services of lecturers, panelists, and other individuals and organizations provided to the Coast Guard on a pro bono basis.
